The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|qq=|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}

== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite book|和書|author=Shibuya Mayuko, Yanagi Makoto, Seichi Ryota|title=Kawarazuka Kofun|date=2019-03-29|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/71238|ncid=BB28934995|doi=10.24484/sitereports.71238|series=Utsunomiya City Archaeological Survey Report No. 104|publisher=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|editor=Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ref={{sfnref|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019}}}}
* {{Cite book|和書|author=Hanawa Shizuo|title=Historical Explorations of Utsunomiya|date=2008-09-27|publisher=Zuisōsha|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|Hanawa|2008}}}}
* {{Cite book|和書|editor=Utsunomiya City|title=Revised History of Utsunomiya|date=1992-03-31|publisher=Utsunomiya City|ncid=BN07977757|ref={{sfnref|Utsunomiya City|1992}}}}

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|qq=|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|qq=|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. | location = {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tochigi Prefecture (1188 Nagaoka Town and other locations){{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|loc=Introduction}}
| coordinates = {{coord|36|36|3.7|N|139|52|54.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}}

| burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in [[:en:1898|1898]] (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in [[:en:1899|1899]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in [[:en:1991|1991]] and [[:en:1992|1992]] by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in [[:en:1995|1995]], and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]. The [[:en:Kawarazuka Kofun|Kawarazuka Kofun]], a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]] to the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]].{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

| location = {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tochigi Prefecture (1188 Nagaoka Town and other locations){{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|loc=Introduction}}
| coordinates = {{coord|36|36|3.7|N|139|52|54.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}}
| type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}
| area = Total length: 48 m, width: 38 m, height: 3.3 m{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=8}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}
| burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun|1=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun|1=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun|1=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|WD=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun|1=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|WD=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|WD=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|WD=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|qid=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun|1=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|qid=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|qid=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|qid=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|qid=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.
