The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|qid=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun|1=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|qid=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|qid=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|qid=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|qid=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.

{{Infobox ancient site}}







{{Otheruses|Kawarazuka Kofun Group in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture|Kawarazuka Kofun in Gyōda, Saitama Prefecture|瓦塚古墳}}
{{Infobox Japanese Kofun
| name = Kawarazuka Kofun
| image = [[File:Kawarazuka Kofun, Utsunomiya. view from west.jpg|280px]]
| caption = View from the west
| alternate_name = 
| part_of = {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun Group|ja|瓦塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun Group|qq=}}{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}
| location = {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Tochigi Prefecture (1188 Nagaoka Town and other locations){{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|loc=Introduction}}
| coordinates = {{coord|36|36|3.7|N|139|52|54.3|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}}
| type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}
| area = Total length: 48 m, width: 38 m, height: 3.3 m{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=8}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}
| burial = {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}
| burial goods = Weapons, horse gear, ornaments, earthenware{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|pp=58-59}}
| associated_tombs = 
| construction = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}
| person = 
| historic_site = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}
| tangible_notes = 
}}

The '''Kawarazuka Kofun Group''' (瓦塚古墳群) is a cluster of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市長岡町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka, Utsunomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun|en|Kawarazuka Kofun|qid=Q11574186|lt=Kawarazuka Kofun|1=Kawarazuka Kofun}}, a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}, is the primary Kofun in this group, which originally consisted of approximately 40 tumuli but now has around 30 due to development and other factors.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=58}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}}

== Overview ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun was first excavated in {{ill|1898|en|1898|qid=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} (Meiji 31). The findings were summarized and published in a scholarly paper by {{ill|Yagi Sōzaburō|ja|八木奘三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Sōzaburō|1=Sōzaburō Yagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q104539772}} in {{ill|1899|en|1899|qid=Q7851|lt=1899|1=1899}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=110}} Following surveys in {{ill|1991|en|1991|qid=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} and {{ill|1992|en|1992|qid=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}} by Sakuin Gakuin High School's social studies research club, the site was Historic Site1 a city historic site in {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}, and local residents initiated preservation activities.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|p=1}}

== Features ==
The Kawarazuka Kofun Group is primarily composed of {{ill|empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/empun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} distributed along the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}} in a layout following the ridgeline. The Kofuns date from the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} to the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}.{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}{{sfn|Utsunomiya City|1992|p=59}} The main tomb, Kawarazuka Kofun, measures 48 m in length and is notable for its double-tier construction, stone mound covering (fukiishi), and rich burial burial goods, including decorative haniwa and earthenware.{{sfn|Shibuya, Yanagi & Seichi|2019|pp=8-9}}{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=111}}

== Surrounding Environment ==
The Utsunomiya Hills area is rich in archaeological sites, including other Kofuns like {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The site is accessible by bus or car, with walking paths leading visitors to the mounds.
